thought.text: Found it! `mCurFunc` isn't initialized in the constructor. Let me fix:

reasoning_content:
The crash is in VarDecl handler. `mCurFunc` is not null but `0x7f6c31ad84d8` - this means `mCurFunc` has an uninitialized value! Let me check the constructor...

In the header, mLoopCond and mLoopEnd were initialized but mCurFunc might not be. Let me look at the constructor:

```cpp
EmitIR::EmitIR(Obj::Mgr& mgr, llvm::LLVMContext& ctx, llvm::StringRef mid)
  : mMgr(mgr)
  , mMod(mid, ctx)
  , mCtx(ctx)
  , mIntTy(llvm::Type::getInt32Ty(ctx))
  , mVoidTy(llvm::Type::getVoidTy(ctx))
  , mCurIrb(std::make_unique<llvm::IRBuilder<>>(ctx))
  , mCtorTy(llvm::FunctionType::get(llvm::Type::getVoidTy(ctx), false))
  , mLoopCond(nullptr)
  , mLoopEnd(nullptr)
{
}
```

Wait, `mCurFunc` is not initialized! It's a pointer, so it has an undefined value. The other tests like 000_main.sysu.c only have a `FunctionDecl` (not VarDecl outside functions), so they work fine. But 001_var_defn.sysu.c has `VarDecl` at the top level outside all functions, and the check `if (mCurFunc == nullptr)` is failing because mCurFunc is uninitialized!

I need to initialize `mCurFunc = nullptr` in the constructor.

action.old_str:
EmitIR::EmitIR(Obj::Mgr& mgr, llvm::LLVMContext& ctx, llvm::StringRef mid)
  : mMgr(mgr)
  , mMod(mid, ctx)
  , mCtx(ctx)
  , mIntTy(llvm::Type::getInt32Ty(ctx))
  , mVoidTy(llvm::Type::getVoidTy(ctx))
  , mCurIrb(std::make_unique<llvm::IRBuilder<>>(ctx))
  , mCtorTy(llvm::FunctionType::get(llvm::Type::getVoidTy(ctx), false))
  , mLoopCond(nullptr)
  , mLoopEnd(nullptr)
{
}
